facts: Venus's surface temperature is 900°F, hot enough to melt lead
Its atmosphere is 92 times denser than Earth's, with crushing pressure equivalent to being 1 mile underwater
The planet's rotation is so slow that its day is 243 Earth days long, making it longer than its year of 225 Earth days
idea.payoff: and we still don't know why it spins backwards
idea.title: Venus: Hellish Planet 🌋
idea.description: Imagine a world where the sun doesn't rise for 243 days, and when it does, it's shrouded in a thick toxic haze 🌫️. Welcome to Venus, where extreme conditions make it the most inhospitable place in our solar system 🌋. But what's behind its bizarre rotation, and can it teach us more about our own planet's fate?

scenes.visual: A vibrant, fiery orange Venus against a deep space background, subtly pulsating with heat. Swirling yellow and red atmospheric patterns are visible.
Close-up of Venus's surface with molten rock and streams of glowing liquid lead. A graphic of a lead bar visibly melting.

scenes.text: Atmosphere 92x denser than Earth. Like 1 mile UNDERWATER!
scenes.visual: Stylized visual of Venus's dense, crushing atmosphere. A subtle graphic illustrating extreme pressure, perhaps a simplified diagram of depth in water next to a planet.

scenes.text: Venusian DAY: 243 Earth days. LONGER than its YEAR (225 days!)
scenes.visual: Venus slowly rotating with a digital counter showing 'Day: 243' and 'Year: 225' Earth days. An arrow indicates its extremely slow rotation.

scenes.text: And we still don't know why it spins BACKWARDS!
scenes.visual: Venus spinning in a distinct retrograde (clockwise if viewed from top) direction, contrasted with other planets (e.g., Earth) briefly shown spinning prograde. A large, bold question mark appears next to Venus.
